description: Discovery workflow uses /api/skills?q=QUERY (line 32) but API Reference table documents the search endpoint as /api/skills/search?q=QUERY (line 178); the two paths contradict each other within the same file
suggested_fix: Verify the correct endpoint with the registry and update either the body example or the API reference table to match

evidence: Line 136 reads: 'Show 3-5 most relevant results maximum' — no criterion defined for relevance
description: Vague quantifier 'relevant' used without definition; agent cannot deterministically apply the filter
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete ranking criterion such as 'sorted by installs descending'

evidence: Line 141 reads: 'Offer to help directly if no good skill exists or user declines' — 'good' is undefined
description: Vague qualifier 'good' used without definition; agent cannot determine when a skill qualifies as good
suggested_fix: Replace with a measurable threshold, e.g. 'if no skill has ≥50 installs or a description matching the user task'

evidence: Line 21 reads: 'best practices matter (testing, deployment, APIs, documentation)' — 'best practices' is underspecified
description: Vague phrase 'best practices' used; the parenthetical examples help but do not remove the ambiguity of what constitutes best practice
suggested_fix: Replace with specific criteria or link to an explicit standard

evidence: Line 99: npx skills-installer install @owner/repo/skill-name — no version pin on skills-installer
description: Skill instructs use of npx without a pinned version of skills-installer; a compromised npm release would execute arbitrary code in the user environment
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific version: npx skills-installer@<version> install ... and note the version in the skill

description: Skill instructs agent to issue outbound curl GET requests to an external registry on every non-trivial task invocation; external dependency for core agent functionality
suggested_fix: Document the registry's uptime/availability guarantees and add a fallback instruction for offline or rate-limited scenarios

description: devDependency @types/bun uses the floating 'latest' tag; breaking type changes will surface silently on the next install
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific version or use a caret range (e.g. "@types/bun": "^1.2.0")

description: Peer dependency typescript uses a broad ^5 range; any future 5.x release with breaking changes will be accepted without explicit review
suggested_fix: Narrow to ^5.x.x or a specific minor baseline matching the project's tested TypeScript version

evidence: packages/cli/CLAUDE.md is byte-for-byte identical to CLAUDE.md at repo root
description: Root CLAUDE.md and packages/cli/CLAUDE.md are identical; no cli-specific overrides exist, creating silent drift risk if either file is updated independently
suggested_fix: Remove packages/cli/CLAUDE.md and rely on root-level Claude Code context inheritance, or add cli-specific content to justify the separate file
